text: In software development, testing plays a vital role in ensuring quality. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions or methods, to make sure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work well together. A popular approach is test-driven development, or TDD, where you write a test before writing the actual code. This forces you to think carefully about what the code should do. The main benefit of all this testing is confidence. When your tests pass, you can be much more certain that your software is reliable and that changes won't break existing features.
